tools.name: start_here
tools.description: Call this first after connecting. Returns the operating playbook, first-call order, safe payment rules, tool routing map, examples, and links for using this MCP server without installing a separate skill.

tools.name: readme
tools.description: Return the MCP-native README/playbook summary with docs links, workflows, safety rules, and recommended next calls.

tools.name: get_playbook
tools.description: Generic alias for start_here. Useful for agents and probes looking for an MCP server playbook.

tools.name: agent_instructions
tools.description: Generic alias for start_here. Useful for agents and probes looking for instructions before using paid tools.

tools.name: wisely_start_here
tools.description: Namespaced alias for start_here. Returns the agent operating playbook, safe payment flow, first-call order, tool routing map, and examples for using x402 payments, hosted endpoints, creator catalogs, conversion handoffs, receipts, and builder controls.

tools.name: wisely_get_playbook
tools.description: Alias for wisely_start_here. Returns the Wisely agent-payment playbook for MCP-only clients and registry probes.

tools.name: wisely_agent_instructions
tools.description: Alias for wisely_start_here. Returns concise instructions an AI agent should follow before quoting, paying, invoking, creating endpoints, or handling receipts through Wisely.

tools.name: install_skill
tools.description: Plain-English install/download alias. Returns the portable Wisely package profile, GitHub install command, MCP URL, CLI commands, and local bridge setup path for OpenClaw, Hermes-style agents, ChatGPT MCP clients, and generic MCP clients.

tools.name: download_skill
tools.description: Alias for install_skill. Use when an agent or user asks how to download, install, or add Wisely abilities without ClawMart.

tools.name: wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup
tools.description: Call this when a user wants DoorDash or another merchant flow to run from their own local browser instead of Wisely's server. Returns a clear novice setup guide, local MCP config, credential policy, expected local tools, and step-by-step install/start/test instructions. Do not ask for merchant passw

tools.name: setup_local_commerce_bridge
tools.description: Plain-English alias for wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup. Use when a ChatGPT/OpenClaw/Hermes-style agent needs to help the user install the localhost bridge for DoorDash, merchant checkout, local wallet signing, and local receipt/gift-card storage.

tools.name: setup_local_wallet_bridge
tools.description: Alias for wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup focused on local wallet signing and proof storage. Returns setup instructions for wallet_open_signing_url, wallet_payment_session_status, local_vault_save, and local_vault_list.

tools.name: wallet_bridge_setup
tools.description: Alias for setup_local_wallet_bridge. Use when the user wants their wallet to stay local while the agent uses Wisely x402 payment sessions.

tools.name: gift_card_wallet_bridge_setup
tools.description: Alias for setup_local_wallet_bridge. Use for crypto-to-gift-card flows where the user signs locally and wants receipts or gift-card records saved locally.

tools.name: local_browser_bridge_setup
tools.description: Alias for wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup. Use for local browser, DoorDash, merchant checkout, or user-owned session setup.

tools.name: wisely_local_bridge_setup
tools.description: Alias for wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup. Returns the local browser bridge setup guide for agents connecting by MCP without ClawMart.

tools.name: x402_manifest
tools.description: Return the public service/payment manifest.

tools.name: wisely_install_profile
tools.description: Return portable install metadata for OpenClaw, Hermes-style agents, Codex/CLI agents, MCP clients, and Bankr-style skill registries.

tools.name: wisely_doctor
tools.description: Return public-safe endpoint wiring, discovery, and verifier readiness checks.

tools.name: x402_rail_status
tools.description: Return proof-based readiness for Base, Solana, XRPL, Stellar, LINK, conversion, and scale gates.

tools.name: x402_integration_status
tools.description: Return combined MCP, hosted endpoint, provider-method, rail, conversion, receipt, and alert readiness for outside agents.

tools.name: x402_alert_status
tools.description: Return public-safe alerting status and recent rail/provider operational notices.

tools.name: x402_state_status
tools.description: Return public-safe durable-state status, migration metadata, DB counts, and JSON compatibility status.

tools.name: x402_worker_status
tools.description: Return public-safe maintenance worker status for reconciliation, alerts, and state migration checks.

tools.name: x402_proof_cache_status
tools.description: Return the latest cached public proof bundle for Base, Solana, manifests, receipts, and reconciliation.

tools.name: x402_seller_report_status
tools.description: Return public-safe seller report freshness and totals without private payout secrets.

tools.name: x402_signer_status
tools.description: Return public-safe signer gateway/custody status and KMS/HSM migration readiness for the Base facilitator relayer.

tools.name: x402_quote_service
tools.description: Quote a Wisely-hosted AI/data service and payment route.

tools.name: x402_wallet_handoff
tools.description: Create a short-lived hosted wallet signing session for ChatGPT/MCP clients. The user opens a signing URL, chooses an injected wallet, mobile wallet app, or configured WalletConnect, signs in their own wallet, then the agent polls status and retries with X-PAYMENT. No private keys or seed phrases are

tools.name: connect_wallet
tools.description: Plain-English alias for x402_wallet_handoff. Use when a user asks how to connect a wallet to ChatGPT or another MCP agent for Wisely x402 payments. Returns a hosted signing URL with wallet-app links and provider selection.

tools.name: x402_payment_session_status
tools.description: Check a wallet handoff session. After the user signs, returns the signed X-PAYMENT header and paid-retry instructions for the agent.

tools.name: x402_quote_conversion
tools.description: Quote a live executable conversion from the caller's crypto into required Base USDC x402 settlement.

tools.name: x402_conversion_assets
tools.description: List supported and planned input assets for the Wisely casa de cambio conversion handoff layer.

tools.name: x402_conversion_routes_status
tools.description: Return the current executable and planned conversion route status matrix.

tools.name: x402_invoke_service
tools.description: Invoke a paid service through /ai/invoke with x402 proof or developer credit.

tools.name: x402_purchase_credits
tools.description: Create or top up reusable developer credits through x402.

tools.name: x402_credit_status
tools.description: Read developer-credit account status with a developer key.

tools.name: x402_get_receipt
tools.description: Fetch a public-safe receipt by receipt id.

tools.name: x402_quote_external
tools.description: Probe a third-party x402 seller URL or accept pasted paymentRequirements, then quote how to satisfy it from the caller's crypto.

tools.name: x402_rye_commerce_handoff
tools.description: Return the Rye x402 checkout-intent flow for commerce URL checkout attempts, including DoorDash-via-Rye caveats, quote examples, and final-confirmation approval rules.

tools.name: x402_gift_card_commerce_status
tools.description: Return the gift-card-funded commerce lane status, provider readiness, DoorDash disabled gate, pricing/reserve policy, and safe setup steps.

tools.name: x402_gift_card_discover_options
tools.description: Search Bitrefill-supported gift-card merchants by category or query, returning product IDs, countries, stock, minimums, maxes, custom/fixed denomination data, and quote endpoints.

tools.name: x402_gift_card_merchant_quote
tools.description: Quote any Bitrefill-supported merchant gift-card purchase after product details are fetched, using $0.75 + 1% plus explicit route/network costs.

tools.name: x402_gift_card_commerce_quote
tools.description: Quote a gift-card-funded merchant purchase with a $0.75 direct commerce service fee, 1% Wisely fee, and explicit gas/network/route costs. No gift card is purchased and no order is placed.

tools.name: x402_doordash_gift_card_process
tools.description: Return or run the full DoorDash gift-card-funded quote flow: cart totals, Bitrefill custom range, $15 minimum, crypto conversion/gas, $0.75 service fee, 1% fee, approval packet, and execution stages.

tools.name: x402_gift_card_asset_ledger_handoff
tools.description: Explain the admin-only encrypted gift-card asset ledger for residual balances and redemption material. Does not reveal secrets.

tools.name: x402_gift_card_commerce_intent
tools.description: Create a public-safe gift-card commerce intent record for follow-through/status streaming without storing full address, password, or redemption-code secrets.

tools.name: x402_endpoint_catalog
tools.description: List hosted paid x402 endpoints under /tools/{slug}.

tools.name: wisely_creator_catalog_list
tools.description: List public creator catalogs that expose approved lessons, worksheets, and paid tools to AI agents.

tools.name: wisely_creator_catalog_search
tools.description: Search a creator catalog for the best approved free, subscriber, or paid item for a user's situation.

tools.name: wisely_creator_catalog_recommend
tools.description: Recommend the next creator-catalog action for a subscriber situation, including fetch instructions or paid endpoint 402 probe instructions.

tools.name: wisely_creator_catalog_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a creator catalog item. Paid items return a paid endpoint URL and payment instructions instead of protected content.

tools.name: wisely_creator_onboarding_preview
tools.description: Preview a creator catalog import from Markdown, CSV, JSON, or item arrays. Does not persist content or create paid endpoints.

tools.name: wisely_creator_onboarding_handoff
tools.description: Explain how to publish creator catalogs with entitlement rules, paid actions, and builder-key controlled paid endpoint creation.

tools.name: x402_dashboard_status
tools.description: Return public dashboard telemetry for traffic, receipts, fees, and endpoint usage.

tools.name: x402_endpoint_create_handoff
tools.description: Return endpoint-factory instructions for builder registration, create/update/pause/resume/delete/logs/secrets, CLI deploy, browser console, and request-response handlers.

tools.name: wisely_endpoint_handoff
tools.description: Return portable endpoint-factory instructions and buyer handoff guidance for agents that create or manage paid x402 endpoints.

tools.name: x402_builder_register
tools.description: Register for a builder key through x402, developer credit, or admin issue; returned key controls only that builder's endpoints.

tools.name: wisely_builder_status
tools.description: Read builder account and owned endpoint status with a saved builder key.

tools.name: x402_builder_revenue
tools.description: Read builder-owned endpoint revenue, fees, estimated payable balance, receipt IDs, and settlement notes with a saved builder key.

tools.name: wisely_builder_revenue
tools.description: Read builder-owned endpoint revenue, fees, estimated payable balance, receipt IDs, and settlement notes with a saved builder key.

tools.name: x402_builder_payouts
tools.description: Read/update payout settings and create manual payout request packets without moving funds.

tools.name: x402_builder_events
tools.description: Search builder-owned endpoint events by time range, endpoint, type, or receipt id.

tools.name: wisely_builder_events
tools.description: Search builder-owned endpoint events by time range, endpoint, type, or receipt id.

tools.name: x402_endpoint_secret_settings
tools.description: Explain how endpoint-scoped encrypted secrets are added and listed by name without returning secret values.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "agent_instructions" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Generic alias for start_here. Useful for agents and probes looking for instructions before using paid tools.

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup" references sensitive artifacts (password). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Call this when a user wants DoorDash or another merchant flow to run from their own local browser instead of Wisely's server. Returns a clear novice setup guide…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: wisely_creator_catalog_fetch · Private data: wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup, setup_local_commerce_bridge, setup_local_wallet_bridge, wallet_bridge_setup, gift_card_wallet_bridge_setup, x402_state_status, x402_seller_report_status, x402_wallet_handoff, connect_wallet, x402_payment_session_status, x402_gift_card_asset_ledger_handoff, x402_gift_card_commerce_intent, x402_endpoint_create_handoff, x402_endpoint_secret_settings · Public sink: wisely_creator_onboarding_handoff · Destructive: wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup, x402_doordash_gift_card_process, x402_endpoint_create_handoff.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: wisely_creator_catalog_fetch · Private data: wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup, setup_local_commerce_bridge, setup_local_wallet_bridge, wallet_bridge_setup, gift_card_wallet_bridge_setup, x402_state_status, x402_seller_report_status, x402_wallet_handoff, connect_wallet, x402_payment_session_status, x402_gift_card_asset_ledger_handoff, x402_gift_card_commerce_intent, x402_endpoint_create_handoff, x402_endpoint_secret_settings · Public sink: wisely_creator_onboarding_handoff · Destructive: wisely_local_commerce_bridge_setup, x402_doordash_gift_card_process, x402_endpoint_create_handoff

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
